message:
feat(session-restore): scrollback replay during session restore

Session restore now optionally replays captured scrollback content into
restored panes, giving operators immediate context without needing to
scroll through empty panes after a restart or crash recovery.

The restore flow loads persisted output segments from the
output_segments table up to each pane's scrollback_checkpoint_seq,
injects them via ScrollbackInjector before sending restore banners
(so banners appear after replayed content), and reports success/failure
counts in both human-readable and JSON output formats.

New --layout-only flag on ft snapshot restore and ft restart skips
scrollback replay when only the mux topology is needed. The
SessionRestoreConfig.restore_scrollback field controls this behavior,
and restore_max_lines caps the volume of replayed content.

RestoredPaneState gains scrollback_checkpoint_seq and last_output_at
fields loaded from the checkpoint's mux_pane_state rows. RestoreSummary
gains scrollback_result (InjectionReport) and scrollback_error fields
with helper methods for counting restored/failed/skipped panes and
total bytes written.

Main CLI changes:
- restore_snapshot_checkpoint() wires layout_only to config
- restore_scrollback_json() and print_scrollback_restore_summary()
  added for JSON and text output paths
- Both robot and human output paths include scrollback summary
- New tests: batch_get_pane_text_returns_requested_panes_with_tail_metadata
  and batch_get_pane_text_isolates_missing_pane_errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
